gimp-airbrush.blurb: Paint in the current brush with varying pressure. Paint application is time-dependent.
gimp-airbrush.help: This tool simulates the use of an airbrush. Paint pressure represents the relative intensity of the paint application. High pressure results in a thicker layer of paint while low pressure results in a thinner layer.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: pressure
args.desc: The pressure of the airbrush strokes (0 <= pressure <= 100)

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-airbrush-default.blurb: Paint in the current brush with varying pressure. Paint application is time-dependent.
gimp-airbrush-default.help: This tool simulates the use of an airbrush. It is similar to 'gimp-airbrush' except that the pressure is derived from the airbrush tools options box. It the option has not been set the default for the option will be used.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-blend.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-edit-blend' instead.
gimp-blend.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-edit-blend' instead.

args.name: gradient-type

args.name: opacity
args.desc: The opacity of the final blend (0 <= opacity <= 100)

args.name: offset
args.desc: Offset relates to the starting and ending coordinates specified for the blend. This parameter is mode dependent. (offset >= 0)

args.name: repeat
args.desc: Repeat mode { REPEAT-NONE (0), REPEAT-SAWTOOTH (1), REPEAT-TRIANGULAR (2), REPEAT-TRUNCATE (3) }

args.name: reverse
args.desc: Use the reverse gradient (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: supersample
args.desc: Do adaptive supersampling (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: max-depth
args.desc: Maximum recursion levels for supersampling (1 <= max-depth <= 9)

args.name: threshold
args.desc: Supersampling threshold (0 <= threshold <= 4)

args.name: dither
args.desc: Use dithering to reduce banding (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: x1
args.desc: The x coordinate of this blend's starting point

args.name: y1
args.desc: The y coordinate of this blend's starting point

args.name: x2
args.desc: The x coordinate of this blend's ending point

args.name: y2
args.desc: The y coordinate of this blend's ending point

gimp-bucket-fill.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-edit-bucket-fill' instead.
gimp-bucket-fill.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-edit-bucket-fill' instead.

args.name: fill-mode
args.desc: The type of fill { BUCKET-FILL-FG (0), BUCKET-FILL-BG (1), BUCKET-FILL-PATTERN (2) }

args.name: paint-mode
args.desc: The paint application mode { LAYER-MODE-NORMAL-LEGACY (0), LAYER-MODE-DISSOLVE (1), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND-LEGACY (2), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY-LEGACY (3), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN-LEGACY (4), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY-LEGACY (5), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE-LEGACY (6), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION-LEGACY (7), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT-LEGACY (8), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY-LEGACY (9), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY-LEGACY (10), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE-LEGACY (11), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION-LEGACY (12), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR-LEGACY (13), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE-LEGACY (14), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE-LEGACY (15), LAYER-MODE-DODGE-LEGACY (16), LAYER-MODE-BURN-LEGACY (17), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT-LEGACY (18), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT-LEGACY (19), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT-LEGACY (20), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE-LEGACY (21), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE-LEGACY (22), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY (23), LAYER-MODE-LCH-HUE (24), LAYER-MODE-LCH-CHROMA (25), LAYER-MODE-LCH-COLOR (26), LAYER-MODE-LCH-LIGHTNESS (27), LAYER-MODE-NORMAL (28), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND (29), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY (30), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN (31), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE (32), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION (33), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT (34), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY (35), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY (36), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE (37), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION (38), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR (39), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE (40), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE (41), LAYER-MODE-DODGE (42), LAYER-MODE-BURN (43), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT (44), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT (45), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT (46), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE (47), LAYER-MODE-VIVID-LIGHT (48), LAYER-MODE-PIN-LIGHT (49), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-LIGHT (50), LAYER-MODE-HARD-MIX (51), LAYER-MODE-EXCLUSION (52), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-BURN (53), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-DARKEN-ONLY (54), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-LIGHTEN-ONLY (55), LAYER-MODE-LUMINANCE (56), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE (57), LAYER-MODE-ERASE (58), LAYER-MODE-MERGE (59), LAYER-MODE-SPLIT (60), LAYER-MODE-PASS-THROUGH (61), LAYER-MODE-REPLACE (62), LAYER-MODE-ANTI-ERASE (63) }

args.name: opacity
args.desc: The opacity of the final bucket fill (0 <= opacity <= 100)

args.name: threshold
args.desc: The threshold determines how extensive the seed fill will be. It's value is specified in terms of intensity levels. This parameter is only valid when there is no selection in the specified image. (0 <= threshold <= 255)

args.name: sample-merged
args.desc: Use the composite image, not the drawable (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: x
args.desc: The x coordinate of this bucket fill's application. This parameter is only valid when there is no selection in the specified image.

args.name: y
args.desc: The y coordinate of this bucket fill's application. This parameter is only valid when there is no selection in the specified image.

gimp-clone.blurb: Clone from the source to the dest drawable using the current brush
gimp-clone.help: This tool clones (copies) from the source drawable starting at the specified source coordinates to the dest drawable. If the "clone_type" argument is set to PATTERN-CLONE, then the current pattern is used as the source and the "src_drawable" argument is ignored. Pattern cloning assumes a tileable pattern and mods the sum of the src coordinates and subsequent stroke offsets with the width and height of the pattern. For image cloning, if the sum of the src coordinates and subsequent stroke offsets exceeds the extents of the src drawable, then no paint is transferred. The clone tool is capable of transforming between any image types including RGB->Indexed--although converting from any type to indexed is significantly slower.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: src-drawable
args.desc: The source drawable

args.name: clone-type
args.desc: The type of clone { CLONE-IMAGE (0), CLONE-PATTERN (1) }

args.name: src-x
args.desc: The x coordinate in the source image

args.name: src-y
args.desc: The y coordinate in the source image

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-clone-default.blurb: Clone from the source to the dest drawable using the current brush
gimp-clone-default.help: This tool clones (copies) from the source drawable starting at the specified source coordinates to the dest drawable. This function performs exactly the same as the 'gimp-clone' function except that the tools arguments are obtained from the clones option dialog. It this dialog has not been activated then the dialogs default values will be used.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-drawable-edit-bucket-fill.blurb: Fill the area by a seed fill starting at the specified coordinates.
gimp-drawable-edit-bucket-fill.help:
This procedure does a seed fill at the specified coordinates, using various parameters from the current context.
In the case of merged sampling, the x and y coordinates are relative to the image's origin; otherwise, they are relative to the drawable's origin.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-opacity', 'gimp-context-set-paint-mode', 'gimp-context-set-foreground', 'gimp-context-set-background', 'gimp-context-set-pattern', 'gimp-context-set-sample-threshold', 'gimp-context-set-sample-merged', 'gimp-context-set-sample-criterion', 'gimp-context-set-diagonal-neighbors', 'gimp-context-set-antialias'.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: fill-type
args.desc: The type of fill { FILL-FOREGROUND (0), FILL-BACKGROUND (1), FILL-WHITE (2), FILL-TRANSPARENT (3), FILL-PATTERN (4) }

args.name: x
args.desc: The x coordinate of this bucket fill's application.

args.name: y
args.desc: The y coordinate of this bucket fill's application.

gimp-drawable-edit-clear.help:
This procedure clears the specified drawable. If the drawable has an alpha channel, the cleared pixels will become transparent. If the drawable does not have an alpha channel, cleared pixels will be set to the background color. This procedure only affects regions within a selection if there is a selection active.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-background'.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable to clear from

gimp-drawable-edit-fill.help:
This procedure fills the specified drawable according to fill mode. This procedure only affects regions within a selection if there is a selection active. If you want to fill the whole drawable, regardless of the selection, use 'gimp-drawable-fill'.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-opacity', 'gimp-context-set-paint-mode', 'gimp-context-set-foreground', 'gimp-context-set-background', 'gimp-context-set-pattern'.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable to fill to

args.name: fill-type
args.desc: The type of fill { FILL-FOREGROUND (0), FILL-BACKGROUND (1), FILL-WHITE (2), FILL-TRANSPARENT (3), FILL-PATTERN (4) }

gimp-drawable-edit-gradient-fill.blurb: Draw a gradient between the starting and ending coordinates with the specified gradient type.
gimp-drawable-edit-gradient-fill.help:
This tool requires information on the gradient type. It creates the specified variety of gradient using the starting and ending coordinates as defined for each gradient type. For shapeburst gradient types, the context's distance metric is also relevant and can be updated with 'gimp-context-set-distance-metric'.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-opacity', 'gimp-context-set-paint-mode', 'gimp-context-set-foreground', 'gimp-context-set-background', 'gimp-context-set-gradient' and all gradient property settings, 'gimp-context-set-distance-metric'.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: gradient-type
args.desc: The type of gradient { GRADIENT-LINEAR (0), GRADIENT-BILINEAR (1), GRADIENT-RADIAL (2), GRADIENT-SQUARE (3), GRADIENT-CONICAL-SYMMETRIC (4), GRADIENT-CONICAL-ASYMMETRIC (5), GRADIENT-SHAPEBURST-ANGULAR (6), GRADIENT-SHAPEBURST-SPHERICAL (7), GRADIENT-SHAPEBURST-DIMPLED (8), GRADIENT-SPIRAL-CLOCKWISE (9), GRADIENT-SPIRAL-ANTICLOCKWISE (10) }

args.name: offset
args.desc: Offset relates to the starting and ending coordinates specified for the blend. This parameter is mode dependent. (offset >= 0)

args.name: supersample
args.desc: Do adaptive supersampling (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: supersample-max-depth
args.desc: Maximum recursion levels for supersampling (1 <= supersample-max-depth <= 9)

args.name: supersample-threshold
args.desc: Supersampling threshold (0 <= supersample-threshold <= 4)

args.name: dither
args.desc: Use dithering to reduce banding (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: x1
args.desc: The x coordinate of this gradient's starting point

args.name: y1
args.desc: The y coordinate of this gradient's starting point

args.name: x2
args.desc: The x coordinate of this gradient's ending point

args.name: y2
args.desc: The y coordinate of this gradient's ending point

gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-item.help:
This procedure strokes the specified item, painting along its outline (e.g. along a path, or along a channel's boundary), with the active paint method and brush, or using a plain line with configurable properties.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-opacity', 'gimp-context-set-paint-mode', 'gimp-context-set-paint-method', 'gimp-context-set-stroke-method', 'gimp-context-set-foreground', 'gimp-context-set-brush' and all brush property settings, 'gimp-context-set-gradient' and all gradient property settings, 'gimp-context-set-line-width' and all line property settings, 'gimp-context-set-antialias'.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable to stroke to

args.name: item
args.desc: The item to stroke

gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-selection.help:
This procedure strokes the current selection, painting along the selection boundary with the active paint method and brush, or using a plain line with configurable properties. The paint is applied to the specified drawable regardless of the active selection.

This procedure is affected by the following context setters: 'gimp-context-set-opacity', 'gimp-context-set-paint-mode', 'gimp-context-set-paint-method', 'gimp-context-set-stroke-method', 'gimp-context-set-foreground', 'gimp-context-set-brush' and all brush property settings, 'gimp-context-set-gradient' and all gradient property settings, 'gimp-context-set-line-width' and all line property settings, 'gimp-context-set-antialias'.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable to stroke to

gimp-edit-blend.blurb: Blend between the starting and ending coordinates with the specified blend mode and gradient type.
gimp-edit-blend.help:
This tool requires information on the paint application mode, the blend mode, and the gradient type. It creates the specified variety of blend using the starting and ending coordinates as defined for each gradient type. For shapeburst gradient types, the context's distance metric is also relevant and can be updated with 'gimp-context-set-distance-metric'.

Deprecated: Use 'gimp-drawable-edit-gradient-fill' instead.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: blend-mode

gimp-edit-bucket-fill.blurb: Fill the area specified either by the current selection if there is one, or by a seed fill starting at the specified coordinates.
gimp-edit-bucket-fill.help:
This tool requires information on the paint application mode, and the fill mode, which can either be in the foreground color, or in the currently active pattern. If there is no selection, a seed fill is executed at the specified coordinates and extends outward in keeping with the threshold parameter. If there is a selection in the target image, the threshold, sample merged, x, and y arguments are unused. If the sample_merged parameter is TRUE, the data of the composite image will be used instead of that for the specified drawable. This is equivalent to sampling for colors after merging all visible layers. In the case of merged sampling, the x and y coordinates are relative to the image's origin; otherwise, they are relative to the drawable's origin.

Deprecated: Use 'gimp-drawable-edit-bucket-fill' instead.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: fill-mode
args.desc: The type of fill { BUCKET-FILL-FG (0), BUCKET-FILL-BG (1), BUCKET-FILL-PATTERN (2) }

args.name: paint-mode
args.desc: The paint application mode { LAYER-MODE-NORMAL-LEGACY (0), LAYER-MODE-DISSOLVE (1), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND-LEGACY (2), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY-LEGACY (3), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN-LEGACY (4), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY-LEGACY (5), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE-LEGACY (6), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION-LEGACY (7), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT-LEGACY (8), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY-LEGACY (9), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY-LEGACY (10), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE-LEGACY (11), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION-LEGACY (12), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR-LEGACY (13), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE-LEGACY (14), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE-LEGACY (15), LAYER-MODE-DODGE-LEGACY (16), LAYER-MODE-BURN-LEGACY (17), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT-LEGACY (18), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT-LEGACY (19), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT-LEGACY (20), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE-LEGACY (21), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE-LEGACY (22), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY (23), LAYER-MODE-LCH-HUE (24), LAYER-MODE-LCH-CHROMA (25), LAYER-MODE-LCH-COLOR (26), LAYER-MODE-LCH-LIGHTNESS (27), LAYER-MODE-NORMAL (28), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND (29), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY (30), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN (31), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE (32), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION (33), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT (34), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY (35), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY (36), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE (37), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION (38), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR (39), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE (40), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE (41), LAYER-MODE-DODGE (42), LAYER-MODE-BURN (43), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT (44), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT (45), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT (46), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE (47), LAYER-MODE-VIVID-LIGHT (48), LAYER-MODE-PIN-LIGHT (49), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-LIGHT (50), LAYER-MODE-HARD-MIX (51), LAYER-MODE-EXCLUSION (52), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-BURN (53), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-DARKEN-ONLY (54), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-LIGHTEN-ONLY (55), LAYER-MODE-LUMINANCE (56), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE (57), LAYER-MODE-ERASE (58), LAYER-MODE-MERGE (59), LAYER-MODE-SPLIT (60), LAYER-MODE-PASS-THROUGH (61), LAYER-MODE-REPLACE (62), LAYER-MODE-ANTI-ERASE (63) }

args.name: opacity
args.desc: The opacity of the final bucket fill (0 <= opacity <= 100)

args.name: threshold
args.desc: The threshold determines how extensive the seed fill will be. It's value is specified in terms of intensity levels. This parameter is only valid when there is no selection in the specified image. (0 <= threshold <= 255)

args.name: sample-merged
args.desc: Use the composite image, not the drawable (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: x
args.desc: The x coordinate of this bucket fill's application. This parameter is only valid when there is no selection in the specified image.

args.name: y
args.desc: The y coordinate of this bucket fill's application. This parameter is only valid when there is no selection in the specified image.

gimp-edit-bucket-fill-full.blurb: Fill the area specified either by the current selection if there is one, or by a seed fill starting at the specified coordinates.
gimp-edit-bucket-fill-full.help:
This tool requires information on the paint application mode, and the fill mode, which can either be in the foreground color, or in the currently active pattern. If there is no selection, a seed fill is executed at the specified coordinates and extends outward in keeping with the threshold parameter. If there is a selection in the target image, the threshold, sample merged, x, and y arguments are unused. If the sample_merged parameter is TRUE, the data of the composite image will be used instead of that for the specified drawable. This is equivalent to sampling for colors after merging all visible layers. In the case of merged sampling, the x and y coordinates are relative to the image's origin; otherwise, they are relative to the drawable's origin.

Deprecated: Use 'gimp-drawable-edit-bucket-fill' instead.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: fill-mode
args.desc: The type of fill { BUCKET-FILL-FG (0), BUCKET-FILL-BG (1), BUCKET-FILL-PATTERN (2) }

args.name: paint-mode
args.desc: The paint application mode { LAYER-MODE-NORMAL-LEGACY (0), LAYER-MODE-DISSOLVE (1), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND-LEGACY (2), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY-LEGACY (3), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN-LEGACY (4), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY-LEGACY (5), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE-LEGACY (6), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION-LEGACY (7), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT-LEGACY (8), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY-LEGACY (9), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY-LEGACY (10), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE-LEGACY (11), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION-LEGACY (12), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR-LEGACY (13), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE-LEGACY (14), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE-LEGACY (15), LAYER-MODE-DODGE-LEGACY (16), LAYER-MODE-BURN-LEGACY (17), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT-LEGACY (18), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT-LEGACY (19), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT-LEGACY (20), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE-LEGACY (21), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE-LEGACY (22), LAYER-MODE-OVERLAY (23), LAYER-MODE-LCH-HUE (24), LAYER-MODE-LCH-CHROMA (25), LAYER-MODE-LCH-COLOR (26), LAYER-MODE-LCH-LIGHTNESS (27), LAYER-MODE-NORMAL (28), LAYER-MODE-BEHIND (29), LAYER-MODE-MULTIPLY (30), LAYER-MODE-SCREEN (31), LAYER-MODE-DIFFERENCE (32), LAYER-MODE-ADDITION (33), LAYER-MODE-SUBTRACT (34), LAYER-MODE-DARKEN-ONLY (35), LAYER-MODE-LIGHTEN-ONLY (36), LAYER-MODE-HSV-HUE (37), LAYER-MODE-HSV-SATURATION (38), LAYER-MODE-HSL-COLOR (39), LAYER-MODE-HSV-VALUE (40), LAYER-MODE-DIVIDE (41), LAYER-MODE-DODGE (42), LAYER-MODE-BURN (43), LAYER-MODE-HARDLIGHT (44), LAYER-MODE-SOFTLIGHT (45), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-EXTRACT (46), LAYER-MODE-GRAIN-MERGE (47), LAYER-MODE-VIVID-LIGHT (48), LAYER-MODE-PIN-LIGHT (49), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-LIGHT (50), LAYER-MODE-HARD-MIX (51), LAYER-MODE-EXCLUSION (52), LAYER-MODE-LINEAR-BURN (53), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-DARKEN-ONLY (54), LAYER-MODE-LUMA-LIGHTEN-ONLY (55), LAYER-MODE-LUMINANCE (56), LAYER-MODE-COLOR-ERASE (57), LAYER-MODE-ERASE (58), LAYER-MODE-MERGE (59), LAYER-MODE-SPLIT (60), LAYER-MODE-PASS-THROUGH (61), LAYER-MODE-REPLACE (62), LAYER-MODE-ANTI-ERASE (63) }

args.name: opacity
args.desc: The opacity of the final bucket fill (0 <= opacity <= 100)

args.name: threshold
args.desc: The threshold determines how extensive the seed fill will be. It's value is specified in terms of intensity levels. This parameter is only valid when there is no selection in the specified image. (0 <= threshold <= 255)

args.name: sample-merged
args.desc: Use the composite image, not the drawable (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: fill-transparent
args.desc: Whether to consider transparent pixels for filling. If TRUE, transparency is considered as a unique fillable color. (TRUE or FALSE)

args.name: select-criterion
args.desc: The criterion used to determine color similarity. SELECT_CRITERION_COMPOSITE is the standard choice. { SELECT-CRITERION-COMPOSITE (0), SELECT-CRITERION-R (1), SELECT-CRITERION-G (2), SELECT-CRITERION-B (3), SELECT-CRITERION-H (4), SELECT-CRITERION-S (5), SELECT-CRITERION-V (6), SELECT-CRITERION-A (7), SELECT-CRITERION-LCH-L (8), SELECT-CRITERION-LCH-C (9), SELECT-CRITERION-LCH-H (10) }

args.name: x
args.desc: The x coordinate of this bucket fill's application. This parameter is only valid when there is no selection in the specified image.

args.name: y
args.desc: The y coordinate of this bucket fill's application. This parameter is only valid when there is no selection in the specified image.

gimp-edit-clear.help:
This procedure clears the specified drawable. If the drawable has an alpha channel, the cleared pixels will become transparent. If the drawable does not have an alpha channel, cleared pixels will be set to the background color. This procedure only affects regions within a selection if there is a selection active.

Deprecated: Use 'gimp-drawable-edit-clear' instead.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable to clear from

gimp-edit-copy.help: If there is a selection in the image, then the area specified by the selection is copied from the specified drawable and placed in an internal GIMP edit buffer. It can subsequently be retrieved using the 'gimp-edit-paste' command. If there is no selection, then the specified drawable's contents will be stored in the internal GIMP edit buffer. This procedure will fail if the selected area lies completely outside the bounds of the current drawable and there is nothing to copy from.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable to copy from

gimp-edit-copy-visible.help: If there is a selection in the image, then the area specified by the selection is copied from the projection and placed in an internal GIMP edit buffer. It can subsequently be retrieved using the 'gimp-edit-paste' command. If there is no selection, then the projection's contents will be stored in the internal GIMP edit buffer.

args.name: image
args.desc: The image to copy from

gimp-edit-cut.help: If there is a selection in the image, then the area specified by the selection is cut from the specified drawable and placed in an internal GIMP edit buffer. It can subsequently be retrieved using the 'gimp-edit-paste' command. If there is no selection, then the specified drawable will be removed and its contents stored in the internal GIMP edit buffer. This procedure will fail if the selected area lies completely outside the bounds of the current drawable and there is nothing to copy from.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable to cut from

gimp-edit-fill.help:
This procedure fills the specified drawable with the fill mode. If the fill mode is foreground, the current foreground color is used. If the fill mode is background, the current background color is used. Other fill modes should not be used. This procedure only affects regions within a selection if there is a selection active. If you want to fill the whole drawable, regardless of the selection, use 'gimp-drawable-fill'.

Deprecated: Use 'gimp-drawable-edit-fill' instead.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable to fill to

args.name: fill-type
args.desc: The type of fill { FILL-FOREGROUND (0), FILL-BACKGROUND (1), FILL-WHITE (2), FILL-TRANSPARENT (3), FILL-PATTERN (4) }

gimp-edit-named-copy.help: This procedure works like 'gimp-edit-copy', but additionally stores the copied buffer into a named buffer that will stay available for later pasting, regardless of any intermediate copy or cut operations.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable to copy from

args.name: buffer-name
args.desc: The name of the buffer to create

gimp-edit-named-copy-visible.blurb: Copy from the projection into a named buffer.
gimp-edit-named-copy-visible.help: This procedure works like 'gimp-edit-copy-visible', but additionally stores the copied buffer into a named buffer that will stay available for later pasting, regardless of any intermediate copy or cut operations.

args.name: image
args.desc: The image to copy from

args.name: buffer-name
args.desc: The name of the buffer to create

gimp-edit-named-cut.help: This procedure works like 'gimp-edit-cut', but additionally stores the cut buffer into a named buffer that will stay available for later pasting, regardless of any intermediate copy or cut operations.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable to cut from

args.name: buffer-name
args.desc: The name of the buffer to create

gimp-edit-named-paste.blurb: Paste named buffer to the specified drawable.
gimp-edit-named-paste.help: This procedure works like 'gimp-edit-paste' but pastes a named buffer instead of the global buffer.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable to paste to

args.name: buffer-name
args.desc: The name of the buffer to paste

args.name: paste-into
args.desc: Clear selection, or paste behind it? (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-edit-named-paste-as-new.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-edit-named-paste-as-new-image' instead.
gimp-edit-named-paste-as-new.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-edit-named-paste-as-new-image' instead.

args.name: buffer-name
args.desc: The name of the buffer to paste

gimp-edit-named-paste-as-new-image.blurb: Paste named buffer to a new image.
gimp-edit-named-paste-as-new-image.help: This procedure works like 'gimp-edit-paste-as-new-image' but pastes a named buffer instead of the global buffer.

args.name: buffer-name
args.desc: The name of the buffer to paste

gimp-edit-paste.blurb: Paste buffer to the specified drawable.
gimp-edit-paste.help: This procedure pastes a copy of the internal GIMP edit buffer to the specified drawable. The GIMP edit buffer will be empty unless a call was previously made to either 'gimp-edit-cut' or 'gimp-edit-copy'. The "paste_into" option specifies whether to clear the current image selection, or to paste the buffer "behind" the selection. This allows the selection to act as a mask for the pasted buffer. Anywhere that the selection mask is non-zero, the pasted buffer will show through. The pasted buffer will be a new layer in the image which is designated as the image floating selection. If the image has a floating selection at the time of pasting, the old floating selection will be anchored to its drawable before the new floating selection is added. This procedure returns the new floating layer. The resulting floating selection will already be attached to the specified drawable, and a subsequent call to floating_sel_attach is not needed.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable to paste to

args.name: paste-into
args.desc: Clear selection, or paste behind it? (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-edit-paste-as-new.blurb: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-edit-paste-as-new-image' instead.
gimp-edit-paste-as-new.help: This procedure is deprecated! Use 'gimp-edit-paste-as-new-image' instead.

gimp-edit-paste-as-new-image.blurb: Paste buffer to a new image.
gimp-edit-paste-as-new-image.help: This procedure pastes a copy of the internal GIMP edit buffer to a new image. The GIMP edit buffer will be empty unless a call was previously made to either 'gimp-edit-cut' or 'gimp-edit-copy'. This procedure returns the new image or -1 if the edit buffer was empty.

gimp-edit-stroke.help:
This procedure strokes the current selection, painting along the selection boundary with the active brush and foreground color. The paint is applied to the specified drawable regardless of the active selection.

Deprecated: Use 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-selection' instead.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable to stroke to

gimp-edit-stroke-vectors.help:
This procedure strokes the specified vectors object, painting along the path with the active brush and foreground color.

Deprecated: Use 'gimp-drawable-edit-stroke-item' instead.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The drawable to stroke to

args.name: vectors
args.desc: The vectors object

gimp-eraser.help: This tool erases using the current brush mask. If the specified drawable contains an alpha channel, then the erased pixels will become transparent. Otherwise, the eraser tool replaces the contents of the drawable with the background color. Like paintbrush, this tool linearly interpolates between the specified stroke coordinates.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

args.name: hardness
args.desc: How to apply the brush { BRUSH-HARD (0), BRUSH-SOFT (1) }

args.name: method
args.desc: The paint method to use { PAINT-CONSTANT (0), PAINT-INCREMENTAL (1) }

gimp-eraser-default.help: This tool erases using the current brush mask. This function performs exactly the same as the 'gimp-eraser' function except that the tools arguments are obtained from the eraser option dialog. It this dialog has not been activated then the dialogs default values will be used.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

gimp-gradient-duplicate.help: This procedure creates an identical gradient by a different name

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

gimp-gradient-get-custom-samples.blurb: Sample the specified gradient in custom positions.
gimp-gradient-get-custom-samples.help: This procedure samples the active gradient in the specified number of points. The procedure will sample the gradient in the specified positions from the list. The left endpoint of the gradient corresponds to position 0.0, and the right endpoint corresponds to 1.0. The procedure returns a list of floating-point values which correspond to the RGBA values for each sample.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: num-samples
args.desc: The number of samples to take (num-samples >= 1)

args.name: positions
args.desc: The list of positions to sample along the gradient

args.name: reverse
args.desc: Use the reverse gradient (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-gradient-get-number-of-segments.blurb: Returns the number of segments of the specified gradient
gimp-gradient-get-number-of-segments.help: This procedure returns the number of segments of the specified gradient.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

gimp-gradient-get-uniform-samples.blurb: Sample the specified in uniform parts.
gimp-gradient-get-uniform-samples.help: This procedure samples the active gradient in the specified number of uniform parts. It returns a list of floating-point values which correspond to the RGBA values for each sample. The minimum number of samples to take is 2, in which case the returned colors will correspond to the { 0.0, 1.0 } positions in the gradient. For example, if the number of samples is 3, the procedure will return the colors at positions { 0.0, 0.5, 1.0 }.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: num-samples
args.desc: The number of samples to take (num-samples >= 2)

args.name: reverse
args.desc: Use the reverse gradient (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-gradient-is-editable.blurb: Tests if gradient can be edited
gimp-gradient-is-editable.help: Returns TRUE if you have permission to change the gradient

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

gimp-gradient-new.help: This procedure creates a new, uninitialized gradient

args.name: name
args.desc: The requested name of the new gradient

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: new-name
args.desc: The new name of the gradient

gimp-gradient-segment-get-blending-funct.blurb: Retrieves the gradient segment's blending function
gimp-gradient-segment-get-blending-funct.help: This procedure retrieves the blending function of the segment at the specified gradient name and segment index.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

gimp-gradient-segment-get-coloring-type.blurb: Retrieves the gradient segment's coloring type
gimp-gradient-segment-get-coloring-type.help: This procedure retrieves the coloring type of the segment at the specified gradient name and segment index.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

gimp-gradient-segment-get-left-color.blurb: Retrieves the left endpoint color of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-get-left-color.help: This procedure retrieves the left endpoint color of the specified segment of the specified gradient.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

gimp-gradient-segment-get-left-pos.blurb: Retrieves the left endpoint position of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-get-left-pos.help: This procedure retrieves the left endpoint position of the specified segment of the specified gradient.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

gimp-gradient-segment-get-middle-pos.blurb: Retrieves the middle point position of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-get-middle-pos.help: This procedure retrieves the middle point position of the specified segment of the specified gradient.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

gimp-gradient-segment-get-right-color.blurb: Retrieves the right endpoint color of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-get-right-color.help: This procedure retrieves the right endpoint color of the specified segment of the specified gradient.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

gimp-gradient-segment-get-right-pos.blurb: Retrieves the right endpoint position of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-get-right-pos.help: This procedure retrieves the right endpoint position of the specified segment of the specified gradient.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

gimp-gradient-segment-range-blend-colors.blurb: Blend the colors of the segment range.
gimp-gradient-segment-range-blend-colors.help: This function blends the colors (but not the opacity) of the segments' range of the gradient. Using it, the colors' transition will be uniform across the range.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

gimp-gradient-segment-range-blend-opacit.blurb: Blend the opacity of the segment range.
gimp-gradient-segment-range-blend-opacit.help: This function blends the opacity (but not the colors) of the segments' range of the gradient. Using it, the opacity's transition will be uniform across the range.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

gimp-gradient-segment-range-delete.help: This function deletes a segment range.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

gimp-gradient-segment-range-flip.help: This function flips a segment range.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

gimp-gradient-segment-range-move.blurb: Move the position of an entire segment range by a delta.
gimp-gradient-segment-range-move.help: This function moves the position of an entire segment range by a delta. The actual delta (which is returned) will be limited by the control points of the neighboring segments.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

args.name: delta
args.desc: The delta to move the segment range (-1 <= delta <= 1)

args.name: control-compress
args.desc: Whether or not to compress the neighboring segments (TRUE or FALSE)

gimp-gradient-segment-range-redistribute.blurb: Uniformly redistribute the segment range's handles
gimp-gradient-segment-range-redistribute.help: This function redistributes the handles of the specified segment range of the specified gradient, so they'll be evenly spaced.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

gimp-gradient-segment-range-replicate.help: This function replicates a segment range a given number of times. Instead of the original segment range, several smaller scaled copies of it will appear in equal widths.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

args.name: replicate-times
args.desc: The number of times to replicate (2 <= replicate-times <= 20)

gimp-gradient-segment-range-set-blending.blurb: Change the blending function of a segments range
gimp-gradient-segment-range-set-blending.help: This function changes the blending function of a segment range to the specified blending function.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

args.name: blending-function
args.desc: The blending function { GRADIENT-SEGMENT-LINEAR (0), GRADIENT-SEGMENT-CURVED (1), GRADIENT-SEGMENT-SINE (2), GRADIENT-SEGMENT-SPHERE-INCREASING (3), GRADIENT-SEGMENT-SPHERE-DECREASING (4), GRADIENT-SEGMENT-STEP (5) }

gimp-gradient-segment-range-set-coloring.blurb: Change the coloring type of a segments range
gimp-gradient-segment-range-set-coloring.help: This function changes the coloring type of a segment range to the specified coloring type.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

args.name: coloring-type
args.desc: The coloring type { GRADIENT-SEGMENT-RGB (0), GRADIENT-SEGMENT-HSV-CCW (1), GRADIENT-SEGMENT-HSV-CW (2) }

gimp-gradient-segment-range-split-midpoi.blurb: Splits each segment in the segment range at midpoint
gimp-gradient-segment-range-split-midpoi.help: This function splits each segment in the segment range at its midpoint.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

gimp-gradient-segment-range-split-unifor.blurb: Splits each segment in the segment range uniformly
gimp-gradient-segment-range-split-unifor.help: This function splits each segment in the segment range uniformly according to the number of times specified by the parameter.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: start-segment
args.desc: The index of the first segment to operate on (start-segment >= 0)

args.name: end-segment
args.desc: The index of the last segment to operate on. If negative, the selection will extend to the end of the string.

args.name: split-parts
args.desc: The number of uniform divisions to split each segment to (2 <= split-parts <= 1024)

gimp-gradient-segment-set-left-color.blurb: Sets the left endpoint color of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-set-left-color.help: This procedure sets the left endpoint color of the specified segment of the specified gradient.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

args.name: color
args.desc: The color to set

args.name: opacity
args.desc: The opacity to set for the endpoint (0 <= opacity <= 100)

gimp-gradient-segment-set-left-pos.blurb: Sets the left endpoint position of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-set-left-pos.help:
This procedure sets the left endpoint position of the specified segment of the specified gradient. The final position will be between the position of the middle point to the left to the middle point of the current segment.
This procedure returns the final position.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

args.name: pos
args.desc: The position to set the guidepoint to (0 <= pos <= 1)

gimp-gradient-segment-set-middle-pos.blurb: Sets the middle point position of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-set-middle-pos.help:
This procedure sets the middle point position of the specified segment of the specified gradient. The final position will be between the two endpoints of the segment.
This procedure returns the final position.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

args.name: pos
args.desc: The position to set the guidepoint to (0 <= pos <= 1)

gimp-gradient-segment-set-right-color.blurb: Sets the right endpoint color of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-set-right-color.help: This procedure sets the right endpoint color of the specified segment of the specified gradient.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

args.name: color
args.desc: The color to set

args.name: opacity
args.desc: The opacity to set for the endpoint (0 <= opacity <= 100)

gimp-gradient-segment-set-right-pos.blurb: Sets the right endpoint position of the specified segment
gimp-gradient-segment-set-right-pos.help:
This procedure sets the right endpoint position of the specified segment of the specified gradient. The final position will be between the position of the middle point of the current segment and the middle point of the segment to the right.
This procedure returns the final position.

args.name: name
args.desc: The gradient name

args.name: segment
args.desc: The index of the segment within the gradient (segment >= 0)

args.name: pos
args.desc: The position to set the guidepoint to (0 <= pos <= 1)

gimp-paintbrush.blurb: Paint in the current brush with optional fade out parameter and pull colors from a gradient.
gimp-paintbrush.help: This tool is the standard paintbrush. It draws linearly interpolated lines through the specified stroke coordinates. It operates on the specified drawable in the foreground color with the active brush. The 'fade-out' parameter is measured in pixels and allows the brush stroke to linearly fall off. The pressure is set to the maximum at the beginning of the stroke. As the distance of the stroke nears the fade-out value, the pressure will approach zero. The gradient-length is the distance to spread the gradient over. It is measured in pixels. If the gradient-length is 0, no gradient is used.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: fade-out
args.desc: Fade out parameter (fade-out >= 0)

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

args.name: method
args.desc: The paint method to use { PAINT-CONSTANT (0), PAINT-INCREMENTAL (1) }

args.name: gradient-length
args.desc: Length of gradient to draw (gradient-length >= 0)

gimp-paintbrush-default.blurb: Paint in the current brush. The fade out parameter and pull colors from a gradient parameter are set from the paintbrush options dialog. If this dialog has not been activated then the dialog defaults will be used.
gimp-paintbrush-default.help: This tool is similar to the standard paintbrush. It draws linearly interpolated lines through the specified stroke coordinates. It operates on the specified drawable in the foreground color with the active brush. The 'fade-out' parameter is measured in pixels and allows the brush stroke to linearly fall off (value obtained from the option dialog). The pressure is set to the maximum at the beginning of the stroke. As the distance of the stroke nears the fade-out value, the pressure will approach zero. The gradient-length (value obtained from the option dialog) is the distance to spread the gradient over. It is measured in pixels. If the gradient-length is 0, no gradient is used.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-pencil.blurb: Paint in the current brush without sub-pixel sampling.
gimp-pencil.help: This tool is the standard pencil. It draws linearly interpolated lines through the specified stroke coordinates. It operates on the specified drawable in the foreground color with the active brush. The brush mask is treated as though it contains only black and white values. Any value below half is treated as black; any above half, as white.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-smudge.help: This tool simulates a smudge using the current brush. High pressure results in a greater smudge of paint while low pressure results in a lesser smudge.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: pressure
args.desc: The pressure of the smudge strokes (0 <= pressure <= 100)

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }

gimp-smudge-default.help: This tool simulates a smudge using the current brush. It behaves exactly the same as 'gimp-smudge' except that the pressure value is taken from the smudge tool options or the options default if the tools option dialog has not been activated.

args.name: drawable
args.desc: The affected drawable

args.name: num-strokes
args.desc: Number of stroke control points (count each coordinate as 2 points) (num-strokes >= 2)

args.name: strokes
args.desc: Array of stroke coordinates: { s1.x, s1.y, s2.x, s2.y, ..., sn.x, sn.y }
